items.headline: Major electrical infrastructure upgrade permitted for Porter Drive research facility
items.summary: A significant power infrastructure project has been permitted at 3210 Porter Drive to support new R&D tenant operations, including installation of electrical transformers, switches, and a new conduit line running along Porter Drive to a Hanover Drive substation. The upgrade signals expansion of research and development activity in Palo Alto's commercial corridor.

items.headline: Historic house preservation vs. new development: four townhomes planned at Crescent Avenue
items.summary: A proposal would retain a 960-square-foot historic single-family home while adding four three-story townhomes on the same property. The Heritage Preservation Commission is reviewing the project, which balances historic preservation with new residential development.

items.headline: City Council Amends Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
items.summary: The council approved an amendment to the city's contract with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC regarding large format digital signage on city-owned property. This could affect where and how digital advertisements appear throughout San José.

items.headline: City Seeks Proposals for Encampment Cleanup Services
items.summary: The council reviewed a report on new requests for proposals to contract encampment cleanup services. This represents the city's effort to address homelessness and public space management through specialized vendor services.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project Moves Forward
items.summary: City Council took action on the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project, likely involving infrastructure upgrades at a city transportation or public facility. Details on scope and timeline would be relevant to regular users of this facility.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase with school district on Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations to buy or acquire property at 10480 Finch Avenue, working with the Cupertino Union School District on price and terms. The outcome could affect future development or use of that site.

items.headline: Milpitas Adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The City Council approved a comprehensive master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, which will guide future recreational development and connectivity in this key area of Milpitas. This plan shapes how residents will access green space and outdoor amenities in one of the city's major growth areas.

items.headline: City Council Updates Meeting Procedures and Protocols Handbook
items.summary: The council made changes to its official procedures and protocols handbook, which governs how public meetings are conducted and how residents can participate. While procedural, this could affect public comment processes and meeting accessibility for community members.
